reasoning: Protest events like those tracked by ACLED follow a right-skewed event count distribution (such as Poisson or Negative Binomial) because they are non-negative and subject to sudden bursts or spikes. Due to this right-skewness, the historical mean is typically higher than the median. Thus, in the absence of a strong deterministic upward trend, the probability that a future 30-day period will strictly exceed the historical 30-day average (taken over the preceding 360 days) is consistently less than 50% (historically around 35-45% for country-level conflict data). I estimate the base rate of exceeding the mean to be around 40%. Following the instructions, I have decayed this estimate toward 0.5 for the later resolution dates due to increasing temporal uncertainty.
